title: Light Truck Drivers
description: Drive a light vehicle, such as a truck or van, with a capacity of less than 26,001 pounds Gross Vehicle Weight (GVW), primarily to pick up merchandise or packages from a distribution center and deliver. May load and unload vehicle.

tasks.text: Obey traffic laws and follow established traffic and transportation procedures.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Report any mechanical problems encountered with vehicles.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Verify the contents of inventory loads against shipping papers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect and maintain vehicle supplies and equipment, such as gas, oil, water, tires, lights, or brakes, to ensure that vehicles are in proper working condition.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Read maps and follow written or verbal geographic directions.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Load and unload trucks, vans, or automobiles.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Present bills and receipts and collect payments for goods delivered or loaded.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Maintain records, such as vehicle logs, records of cargo, or billing statements, in accordance with regulations.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Drive vehicles with capacities under three tons to transport materials to and from specified destinations, such as railroad stations, plants, residences, offices, or within industrial yards.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Turn in receipts and money received from deliveries.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Use and maintain the tools or equipment found on commercial vehicles, such as weighing or measuring devices.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Report delays, accidents, or other traffic and transportation situations to bases or other vehicles, using telephones or mobile two-way radios.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform emergency repairs, such as changing tires or installing light bulbs, fuses, tire chains, or spark plugs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
